figures.caption: Fig. 6. Results of NBench. Each group on the x-axis corresponds to a benchmark, showing both the native and enclave versions. The y-axis shows the performance of each variant normalized to the native performance. The left graph shows the comparison for CCX on Arm hardware, while the right shows the results for SGX on Intel hardware.
TABLE VI COMPARATIVE TABLE OF RELATED WORK. A FULL CIRCLE INDICATES COMPLETE, AND A HALF CIRCLE PARTIAL SUPPORT FOR A FEATURE.

TABLE VII THE FOLLOWING TABLE PRESENTS AN OVERVIEW OF THE SGX ENCLU AND ENCLS MICROINSTRUCTIONS. THE LEAF-NUMBER IN THE EAX REGISTER DECIDES THE MICROPROGRAMS TO BE RUN. A SHORT DESCRIPTION FOR EACH PROGRAM IS GIVEN IN THE LAST COLUMN.
TABLE VIII RESULTS OF THE MICROBENCHMARKS FOR THE CCX MICROPROGRAMS WITH THE RESULTS IN NANOSECONDS.
Fig. 5. Results of the microbenchmarks for the enclave microprograms. The y-axis shows the runtime of each microprogram for CCX in nanoseconds on a logarithmic scale.

Fig. 3. Overview of CCX. Trusted components are gray; untrusted components are white. The CCX-Service primarily includes the microprogram emulator provided by the CCX-Core and supporting components operating within the TF-A.
